concepts.description: Backpropagation is the algorithm used to efficiently compute the gradients of the loss function with respect to the weights of the network, applying the chain rule layer by layer.
concepts.name: Backpropagation

concepts.description: These are the foundational architecture in neural networks, where information flows in one direction from input to output layers without cycles.
concepts.name: Feedforward Networks

concepts.description: This is an optimization algorithm used to train neural networks, where model parameters are updated using gradients computed from small random subsets of the training data called mini-batches.
concepts.name: Stochastic Gradient Descent with Mini-batches

concepts.description: Batch normalization is a technique that normalizes the inputs of each layer, helping to stabilize and speed up the training of deep neural networks by mitigating vanishing/exploding gradients and allowing higher learning rates.
concepts.name: Batch Normalization

concepts.description: Functions like ReLU, sigmoid, and tanh are applied to the output of each neuron to introduce non-linearity, enabling neural networks to learn complex patterns.
concepts.name: Activation Functions

concepts.description: The softmax function is used in the output layer of a neural network for multi-class classification, converting raw scores into probabilities that sum to one.
concepts.name: Softmax Output

concepts.description: Cross-entropy loss is a common loss function used in classification tasks, measuring the performance of a classification model whose output is a probability value between 0 and 1.
concepts.name: Cross-Entropy Loss

concepts.description: Methods such as L2 weight decay and dropout are employed to prevent neural networks from overfitting the training data, improving their generalization ability to unseen examples.
concepts.name: Regularization Techniques

concepts.description: These are issues encountered during training deep neural networks where gradients become either extremely small (vanishing) or extremely large (exploding), hindering effective learning.
concepts.name: Vanishing and Exploding Gradients
